chief_complaint: A 56-day-old female infant presented with polyuria, fever, vomiting, and dehydration.
history: The patient was diagnosed with neonatal diabetes mellitus (NDM) and had a clinical presentation that included diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA). Initial treatment with subcutaneous insulin was poorly controlled. A KCNJ11 mutation was later identified, and the patient was switched to sulfonylurea for better blood sugar control.
exam: Vital signs: fever, dehydration. Physical examination findings: polyuria.
labs: Laboratory workup indicated diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) with hyperglycemia, ketosis, and metabolic acidosis.
correct_diagnosis: De novo KCNJ11 mutation in an infant with neonatal diabetes mellitus presenting as diabetic ketoacidosis
differentials: De novo KCNJ11 mutation in an infant with neonatal diabetes mellitus presenting as diabetic ketoacidosis
Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) due to other causes
Neonatal diabetes mellitus (NDM) due to other genetic mutations
correct_ranking: De novo KCNJ11 mutation in an infant with neonatal diabetes mellitus presenting as diabetic ketoacidosis
Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) due to other causes
Neonatal diabetes mellitus (NDM) due to other genetic mutations
